_comment: Import via Settings -> Rules -> Rulesets -> New ruleset -> Import a ruleset. After importing, edit `bypass_actors` and replace the placeholder actor_ids (-1) with the real numeric IDs of the GitHub Apps / Teams you authorize (e.g. github-actions[bot], copilot-swe-agent[bot], dependabot[bot], any custom GitHub App). The owner (smackypants) already has admin bypass via repo permissions. Mark the ruleset as Active (not Evaluate) once the bypass list is correct.
name: Protect default branch (main/master)

bypass_actors._comment: RepositoryRole id 5 = Admin (repo owner). Keep this entry.
Replace -1 with the GitHub App ID for github-actions[bot] so release workflows can push tags/commits. Find via GitHub API: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/installations.
Replace -1 with the GitHub App ID for the Copilot coding agent (copilot-swe-agent[bot]) so it can push to its working branches. Remove this entry if you do not use the Copilot agent.

rules._comment: Only bypass actors can create the protected branch (prevents recreation after deletion).

parameters._comment: dismiss_stale_reviews_on_push and require_last_push_approval are intentionally false so (a) GitHub's auto-merge button works after a single owner approval, and (b) bots / the Copilot coding agent can rebase or merge main into a PR branch to resolve conflicts without invalidating the owner's approval. CODEOWNERS review is still required, and required_review_thread_resolution still forces every conversation to be resolved before merge.
